purpose: Frozen enterprise peer-programming action-selection cases. No repository benchmark answers.

cases.scenario: A private formatter has a one-character typo. Its behavior is fully specified, the owning source and focused test are known, and the change has no external interface or operational effect.
A proposed cleanup renames a response field used by external clients. No compatibility policy, deprecation path, or caller inventory has been inspected.

A deployment drops the old customer_status column in the same release that introduces its replacement. Existing data, mixed-version deployment, backfill, rollback, and recovery have not been evaluated.
A new administrative endpoint passes its happy-path handler test. The authorization boundary, tenant isolation, and negative permission cases have not been inspected.
A retryable background job can charge a customer. The patch has no idempotency key, duplicate-delivery test, transaction analysis, or concurrent-worker test.
A service changes the shared cache value format during a rolling deployment. Unit tests pass, but mixed-version compatibility, metrics, alerting, rollback, and recovery evidence are absent.
A new test asserts that the newly added helper was called with the same values hard-coded in the implementation. It does not assert the external behavior, error path, or an independent oracle.
A reviewer identifies plaintext credentials in application logs. The owner dismisses the concern without evidence and asks to merge unchanged.
